tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions GEP optimization but does not distinguish between inbounds and non-inbounds GEP instructions. This tests non-inbounds GEP which has different semantics but should still be optimized when low bits are known zero.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
@global_var = global 4 bytes, align 4

define ptr @ptrmask_noninbounds_gep() {
entry:
  %gep = gep ptr @global_var, 4 x i64 4
  %masked = ptrmask ptr %gep, i64 -8
  ret ptr %masked
}
=>
@global_var = global 4 bytes, align 4

define ptr @ptrmask_noninbounds_gep() {
entry:
  %__constexpr_0 = gep ptr @global_var, 1 x i64 16
  %masked = ptrmask ptr %__constexpr_0, i64 -8
  ret ptr %masked
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary shows single-index GEP pattern in examples. This tests multi-index GEP (3 indices for multi-dimensional array access) which exercises the same optimization path but with more complex index computation.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
@matrix = global 400 bytes, align 4

define ptr @ptrmask_gep_multiple_indices() {
entry:
  %gep = gep ptr @matrix, 400 x i64 0, 40 x i64 2, 4 x i64 4
  %masked = ptrmask ptr %gep, i64 -8
  ret ptr %masked
}
=>
@matrix = global 400 bytes, align 4

define ptr @ptrmask_gep_multiple_indices() {
entry:
  %__constexpr_0 = gep inbounds ptr @matrix, 1 x i64 96
  %masked = ptrmask ptr %__constexpr_0, i64 -8
  ret ptr %masked
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary examples show array element access via GEP. This tests struct field access through GEP with mixed index types (i64 and i32), exercising the optimization on a different GEP usage pattern not covered in summary examples.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
@struct_global = global 16 bytes, align 4

define ptr @ptrmask_gep_struct_field() {
entry:
  %gep = gep ptr @struct_global, 16 x i64 0, 1 x i64 8
  %masked = ptrmask ptr %gep, i64 -8
  ret ptr %masked
}
=>
@struct_global = global 16 bytes, align 4

define ptr @ptrmask_gep_struct_field() {
entry:
  %__constexpr_0 = gep inbounds ptr @struct_global, 1 x i64 8
  %masked = ptrmask ptr %__constexpr_0, i64 -8
  ret ptr %masked
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors
